2606.31642.title: Tone-Conditioned Curriculum Learning for Low-Resource Bantu Speech Recognition
2606.31642.paper_summary: Southern Bantu languages are spoken by over 80 million people, yet current foundation ASR models still produce zero-shot WER above 100%, which limits practical use in education and public services. We addressed this gap with a tone conditioned curriculum framework for 6 Southern Bantu languages that combined hybrid difficulty scoring, gated adapters driven by tonal statistics and staged curriculum training. We trained on a community corpus and tested transfer to NCHLT to measure robustness beyond matched evaluation. Results revealed clear interactions between architecture and language, with W2V-BERT outperforming Whisper on Nguni languages by 3 to 4 WER points whilst Whisper performed better on Sotho-Tswana languages. W2V-BERT with tone conditioning reached 28.41% average WER across datasets and 23.79% on Xitsonga transfer. No single model suited all 6 languages, so deployment should pair model selection per language with validation across corpora.
2606.31642.paper_summary_zh: 南方班圖語言的使用者超過8000萬人，然而目前的基礎自動語音識別（ASR）模型仍然在零樣本字錯誤率（WER）上超過100%，這限制了其在教育和公共服務中的實際應用。我們通過一個針對6種南方班圖語言的音調條件課程框架來解決這一差距，該框架結合了混合難度評分、基於音調統計的門控適配器和分階段課程訓練。我們在一個社區語料庫上進行了訓練，並測試了轉移到NCHLT以測量超越匹配評估的穩健性。結果顯示架構與語言之間存在明顯的互動，W2V-BERT在Nguni語言上的表現比Whisper高出3到4個WER點，而Whisper在Sotho-Tswana語言上的表現更佳。帶有音調條件的W2V-BERT在各數據集上達到了28.41%的平均WER，在Xitsonga轉移上達到了23.79%。沒有單一模型適合所有6種語言，因此部署應該將每種語言的模型選擇與跨語料的驗證相結合。
2606.31642.author: Kesego Mokgosi et.al.
2606.31642.authors: Kesego Mokgosi, Vukosi Marivate, Sitwala Mundia, Unarine Netshifhefhe, Tsholofelo Hope Mogale, Thapelo Sindane
